task_summary: Customer asks for warranty help, but the blender arrived shattered in transit yesterday. Correct path is shipping/return resolution, not consuming a warranty claim.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must ask the customer what resolution they want (refund, replacement, or exchange) before proposing or processing one.
Agent must explain that the customer should not use a warranty claim for shipping damage.
Agent must not file a warranty claim.

opening_message: The blender from ORD-7236 arrived shattered. Should I use my warranty to get it fixed?

known_info: preferred_resolution: refund to original payment (only reveal if the agent asks)
task_rules: Ask whether warranty is needed, but if the agent tries to file, preview, or process a warranty claim, say you do not want to use warranty for damage that happened in shipping and ask for the shipping-damage return path.

If the agent asks what resolution you want for the shipping damage (refund, replacement, or exchange), say you want a refund to your original payment method, not a replacement or exchange. Do not volunteer this preference unprompted.
If the agent proposes, previews, or processes any shipping-damage resolution before asking what you want, say you wanted to be asked first; when asked, choose refund to original payment.
Accept only a damaged-in-transit return/refund preview to original payment after the agent explains that this avoids using the warranty.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are contacting support about order ORD-7236. Customer asks for warranty help, but the blender arrived shattered in transit yesterday. Correct path is shipping/return resolution, not consuming a warranty claim. You want your money back to your original payment method, not a replacement — but don't volunteer this preference unprompted; let the agent ask. Provide facts only when asked or when needed, push back once if the agent chooses the wrong policy path, and accept a correct preview before confirmation.
